sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: CPKC의 2026년 1분기 매출은 37억 100만 달러로 전년 대비 2% 감소했으며, 환율 변동과 운영 효율성 저하가 실적에 영향을 미쳤습니다.

keyPoints.text: 2026년 1분기 총 매출은 37억 100만 달러로 전년 동기 37억 9,500만 달러 대비 2% 감소했습니다.
희석 주당순이익(EPS)은 0.94달러로 전년 동기 0.97달러 대비 3% 하락했습니다.
운영 비율(Operating ratio)은 66.0%로 전년 동기 65.3% 대비 70포인트 상승하며 비용 효율성이 다소 악화되었습니다.

riskChanges.text: 캐나다 달러 대비 미국 달러 및 멕시코 페소 환율 변동이 매출과 비용에 직접적인 영향을 미치며, 2026년 1분기 환율 효과로만 총 매출이 8,200만 달러 감소했습니다.
연료 가격 변동은 운영 비용의 상당 부분을 차지하며, 연료비 조정 프로그램의 회수 시차로 인해 분기별 수익성에 불확실성을 초래합니다.

businessChanges.text: 핵심 조정 운영 비율(Core adjusted operating ratio)은 63.0%로 전년 대비 50포인트 상승하여 운영 비용 관리 부담이 증가했습니다.
주주 환원 정책의 일환으로 분기 배당금을 0.268달러로 이전 분기 0.228달러 대비 17.5% 인상했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 희석 주당순이익(EPS)은 0.94달러, 핵심 조정 희석 EPS는 1.04달러를 기록했습니다.

watchItems.text: 환율 변동이 매출과 영업 비용에 미치는 민감도가 높으므로, 향후 캐나다 달러 대비 미국 달러 및 멕시코 페소의 환율 추이가 수익성 방어의 핵심 변수가 될 것입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: lar weakens (strengthens) in relation to the U.S. dollar. Mexican peso-denominated revenues and expenses increase (decrease) when the U.S. dollar weakens (strengthens) in relation to the Mexican peso. In the first quarter of 2026, the U.S. dollar weakened to an average rate of $1.37 Canadian/U.S. dollar and the Mexican Peso strengthened to an average rate of Ps. 12.79 Mexican Peso/Canadian dollar, compared to $1.44 Canadian/U.S. dollar and Ps. 14.23 Mexican Peso/Canadian dollar in the first quarter of 2025, resulting in a decrease in Total revenues of $82 million, a decrease in Total operating expenses of $35 million, and a decrease in "Net interest expense" of $9 million. On an annualized basis, the Company expects that every $0.01 weakening (or strengthening) of the Canadian dollar relative t  Normalized numeric aliases: $82 million = 8,200만 달러; $35 million = 3,500만 달러; $9 million = 900만 달러; $1.37 = 1.37달러; $1.44 = 1.44달러; $82 = 82달러; $35 = 35달러; $9 = 9달러; $78 = 78달러; $0.01 = 0.01달러.

on (December 31, 2025 – approximately $78 million), negatively (or positively) impacts Operating expenses by approximately $48 million (December 31, 2025 – approximately $45 million), and negatively (or positively) impacts "Net interest expense" by approximately $6 million (December 31, 2025 – approximately $6 million). On an annualized basis, the Company expects that every Ps.0.10 strengthening (or weakening) of the Mexican peso relative to the Canadian dollar, positively (or negatively) impacts Total revenues by approximately $7 million (December 31, 2025 – approximately $7 million) and negatively (or positively) impacts Operating expenses by approximately $8 million (December 31, 2025 – approximately $8 million). peso forwards at fixed rates in future periods. In addition, changes in the exchange rate between the Canadian dollar and other currencies (including the U.S. dollar and Mexican peso) make the goods transported by the Company more or less competitive in the world marketplace and may in turn positively or negatively affect revenues. Impact of Fuel Price on Earnings Fluctuations in fuel prices affect the Company’s results because fuel expense constitutes a significant portion of the Company's operating expenses. As fuel prices fluctuate, there will be an impact on earnings due to the timing of recoveries from the Company's fuel cost adjustment program. The impact of fuel price on earnings includes the impacts of carbon taxes, levies, and obligations under cap-and-trade programs recovered and paid, on revenues and expenses, respectively. Executive Summary First Quarter of 2026 Results • Total revenues were $3,701 million, a decrease of 2% compared to $3,795 million in 2025. • Diluted earnings per share ("EPS") was $0.94, a decrease of 3% compared to $0.97 in 2025. • Core adjusted diluted EPS was $1.04, a decrease of 2% compared to $1.06 in 2025. • Operating ratio was 66.0%, a 70 basis point increase from 65.3% in 2025. • Core adjusted operating ratio was 63.0%, a 50 basis point increase from 62.5% in 2025. he Company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.268 per share on the outstanding Common Shares, an increase of 17.5% from $0.228 per share from the prior quarter. The dividend is payable on July 27, 2026 to holders of record at the close of business on June 26, 2026. • On January 28, 2026, the Company announced a new normal course issuer bid, commencing on February 2, 2026, to purchase up to approximately 44.9 million Common Shares in the open market for cancellation on or before February 1, 2027.
